title: Couldn't move process 6845 to requested cgroup
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
I try to run about 100 containers with podman ~~and start them with systemd~~.
~~Every container runs systemd inside~~. If i reboot the server, all containers are started,
but i mostly get 3 to 8 failed systemd units (libpod-xxxxx.scope).


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install podman 1.8.0 from source

2. Create a container image ~~which runs systemd inside~~

3. Create systemd service files for about 100 containers and enable them for startup

4. Reboot the container host.

**Describe the results you received:**


```
systemctl --failed
  UNIT                                                                          LOAD   ACTIVE SUB    DESCRIPTION                                                                            
+ libpod-19cc1cee40bbc38377d5042a1c6589746ef4bf676f3962fa044dd9a4aa83af85.scope loaded failed failed libcontainer container 19cc1cee40bbc38377d5042a1c6589746ef4bf676f3962fa044dd9a4aa83af85
+ libpod-44b50ce553d836fc36ca378ab06a6751eafc1f983e365c4532d32a6d0734c1a8.scope loaded failed failed libcontainer container 44b50ce553d836fc36ca378ab06a6751eafc1f983e365c4532d32a6d0734c1a8
+ libpod-6c5a6d904abe0250fa4901ea15bb0bbd1e0694d5b6626608157c19af19dd86d5.scope loaded failed failed libcontainer container 6c5a6d904abe0250fa4901ea15bb0bbd1e0694d5b6626608157c19af19dd86d5

LOAD   = Reflects whether the unit definition was properly loaded.
ACTIVE = The high-level unit activation state, i.e. generalization of SUB.
SUB    = The low-level unit activation state, values depend on unit type.

3 loaded units listed. Pass --all to see loaded but inactive units, too.
To show all installed unit files use 'systemctl list-unit-files'.
```

Here the output of journal -u  container-web-webXXXX.service

```
Feb 20 09:17:45 n1.cc1.example.com systemd[1]: libpod-19cc1cee40bbc38377d5042a1c6589746ef4bf676f3962fa044dd9a4aa83af85.scope: Failed to set blkio.weight: No such file or directory
Feb 20 09:17:45 n1.cc1.example.com systemd[1]: libpod-19cc1cee40bbc38377d5042a1c6589746ef4bf676f3962fa044dd9a4aa83af85.scope: Couldn't move process 6845 to requested cgroup '/machine.slice/libpod-19cc1cee40bbc38377d5042a1c6589746ef4bf676f3962fa044dd9a4aa83af85.scope': No such process
Feb 20 09:17:45 n1.cc1.example.com systemd[1]: libpod-19cc1cee40bbc38377d5042a1c6589746ef4bf676f3962fa044dd9a4aa83af85.scope: Failed to add PIDs to scope's control group: No such process
Feb 20 09:17:45 n1.cc1.example.com systemd[1]: libpod-19cc1cee40bbc38377d5042a1c6589746ef4bf676f3962fa044dd9a4aa83af85.scope: Failed with result 'resources'.
Feb 20 09:17:45 n1.cc1.example.com systemd[1]: libpod-19cc1cee40bbc38377d5042a1c6589746ef4bf676f3962fa044dd9a4aa83af85.scope: Job libpod-19cc1cee40bbc38377d5042a1c6589746ef4bf676f3962fa044dd9a4aa83af85.scope/start finished, result=failed
Feb 20 09:17:45 n1.cc1.example.com systemd[1]: Failed to start libcontainer container 19cc1cee40bbc38377d5042a1c6589746ef4bf676f3962fa044dd9a4aa83af85.
Feb 20 09:17:45 n1.cc1.example.com systemd[1]: libpod-19cc1cee40bbc38377d5042a1c6589746ef4bf676f3962fa044dd9a4aa83af85.scope: Unit entered failed state.
```

The container will start, but it will stay in the cgroup of systemd service.


**Describe the results you expected:**

```
systemctl --failed
0 loaded units listed. Pass --all to see loaded but inactive units, too.
To show all installed unit files use 'systemctl list-unit-files'.
```
Every container should be in his own cgroup e.g libpod-containerid.scope


**Output of `podman version`:**


```
Version:            1.8.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.12
Built:              Thu Feb 20 10:38:27 2020
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.12.12
  podman version: 1.8.0
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.13.1

  Conmon:
    package: example-podman-1.8.0_7-1.x86_64

    version: 'conmon version 2.0.10, commit: unknown'
  Distribution:
    distribution: '"centos"'
    version: "8"
  MemFree: 6729531392
  MemTotal: 8191901696
  OCIRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: runc-1.0.0-64.rc9.module_el8.1.0+272+3e64ee36.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  SwapFree: 2147479552
  SwapTotal: 2147479552

  cpus: 4

  hostname: n1.cc1.example.com

  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
example-podman-1.8.0_7-1.x86_64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

systemd service file

```
[Unit]
Description=container-web-web8101
StartLimitIntervalSec=120
StartLimitBurst=6
Wants=container-sssd.service
After=container-sssd.service

[Service]
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=2
ExecStartPre=rm -f %t/%n.pid %t/%n.cid
ExecStart=podman run --conmon-pidfile %t/%n.pid --cidfile %t/%n.cid --log-driver journald --log-opt tag="{{.Name}}" --cgroups=no-conmon --rm -d --name web-web8101 --systemd always --env-file /data/srv/web/web8101/meta/Envfile -v /data/srv/web/web8101/volumes/secrets:/usr/local/secrets:Z -v /data/srv/web/web8101/volumes/data:/data:Z -v /data/srv/web/web8101/volumes/spool:/var/spool:Z -v /data/srv/web/web8101/volumes/tmp:/var/tmp:Z -v /data/srv/space/volumes/web/web8101/web:/web:z -v /data/srv/sssd/volumes/sss/pipes:/var/lib/sss/pipes:z -v /data/srv/sssd/volumes/sss/mc:/var/lib/sss/mc:ro,z localhost/example/php-centos8
ExecStartPost=/opt/example-cc/libexec/regsvc --service-file /data/srv/web/web8101/meta/run.yaml --tries 5
ExecStop=/opt/example-cc/libexec/regsvc --unregister --service-file /data/srv/web/web8101/meta/run.yaml --tries 5
ExecStop=bash -c "podman stop $(cat %t/%n.cid)"
ExecStopPost=bash -c "podman stop $(cat %t/%n.cid) 2> /dev/null || true"
KillMode=none
Type=forking
PIDFile=%t/%n.pid

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```

comments.body:
I did another test run. this time with a dummy container image without systemd.

Build:
```
FROM docker.io/centos:8

CMD exec /bin/sh -c "trap : TERM INT; (while true; do sleep 1000; done) & wait"
```
systemd service

```
[Unit]
Description=container-dummy-web8100

[Service]
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=10
ExecStartPre=rm -f %t/%n.pid %t/%n.cid
ExecStart=podman run --conmon-pidfile %t/%n.pid --cidfile %t/%n.cid --log-driver journald --log-opt tag="{{.Name}}" --name dummy-web8100 --rm -d -t localhost/example/dummy-centos8
ExecStop=bash -c "podman stop -t 1 $(cat %t/%n.cid)"
ExecStopPost=bash -c "podman stop -t 1 $(cat %t/%n.cid) || true"
KillMode=none
Type=forking
PIDFile=%t/%n.pid

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

```

The result is the same and i still get about same amount of errors

```
systemctl --failed
  UNIT                                                                          LOAD   ACTIVE SUB    DESCRIPTION                                                                            
● libpod-0d7af6133f0d842949d8ac049b18dee36d62e991ec793f64553d93aa1cc81db3.scope loaded failed failed libcontainer container 0d7af6133f0d842949d8ac049b18dee36d62e991ec793f64553d93aa1cc81db3
● libpod-31244a80fe368ce31d3766f78a15fd2dcd9f999d74bebadb45f2a52f7e736e06.scope loaded failed failed libcontainer container 31244a80fe368ce31d3766f78a15fd2dcd9f999d74bebadb45f2a52f7e736e06
● libpod-5981a4a82bd1cab57dcfc8e3ff983b97e724b23109ebfae5be43ccc937f7e1c4.scope loaded failed failed libcontainer container 5981a4a82bd1cab57dcfc8e3ff983b97e724b23109ebfae5be43ccc937f7e1c4
● libpod-9e927e0b9baa929b7b6eff0ce468e59a302026f5f1be09a2d43400a40c5518be.scope loaded failed failed libcontainer container 9e927e0b9baa929b7b6eff0ce468e59a302026f5f1be09a2d43400a40c5518be
● libpod-a3df58f9d288dc57a41c6f00b6d0ac1b054bbaa6ac3eae9245970a161aa06826.scope loaded failed failed libcontainer container a3df58f9d288dc57a41c6f00b6d0ac1b054bbaa6ac3eae9245970a161aa06826
● libpod-b16f18920ce350d9eddd3f6ab65af07dd5aa2e24c4936099fc09305182207f8f.scope loaded failed failed libcontainer container b16f18920ce350d9eddd3f6ab65af07dd5aa2e24c4936099fc09305182207f8f
● libpod-bedd8c420094d03acba03ef9b68b626ba0557da1d1e4031c9aff973ae237dfb6.scope loaded failed failed libcontainer container bedd8c420094d03acba03ef9b68b626ba0557da1d1e4031c9aff973ae237dfb6
● libpod-f4020282c0ebed415490915b5a9659216b64a24644f8c70ab1075fd212741391.scope loaded failed failed libcontainer container f4020282c0ebed415490915b5a9659216b64a24644f8c70ab1075fd212741391
● libpod-ff3bc8edf710dd4aec274262320554e15391188d964ca17eb219d595e0a53d89.scope loaded failed failed libcontainer container ff3bc8edf710dd4aec274262320554e15391188d964ca17eb219d595e0a53d89

```

A workaround for this problem is to use cgroupfs as cgroup-manager, so systemd is out of the loop regarding cgroups. 

```
podman --cgroup-manager cgroupfs run ...
podman --cgroup-manager cgroupfs stop ...
```
Every container has its own libpod-... cgroup and i can set memory limits.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@mheon @giuseppe Do you guys have any thoughts on this one?  I don't know if anyone ever looked at it?

Sounds like some sort of race inside runc, maybe?

Trying crun might be interesting.
@ah83 Is this still an issue?  Have you tried this with crun?  Since we have not heard a comment since April, I will take it that it is ok.  Reopen if I am mistaken.
